title: TaskManager: reverse-dependency sync is dead code + broken-by-design
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness (1) / Cognitive Readability (4)

Location: src/session/task.rs:279-336 (update_task second pass).

Violation: The code block at lines 279-305 has a genuine bug that's been papered over with a 'suppress unused warning' comment:

    if let Some(s) = status {
        let _ = s; // suppress unused warning
    }

This exists because the author intended to re-use the original add_blocks/add_blocked_by arguments after the borrow ended, but they've been moved into the destructuring at line 196. The comment admits this: 'Actually we need the original add_blocks/add_blocked_by args, but they've been moved.'

The 'second pass' (lines 308-335) then walks task.blocks and task.blocked_by from the target task and attempts to sync the reverse relationship. This works for newly-added edges IN THIS CALL but ALSO re-walks every pre-existing edge on every update — for a task with N blocks, every update_task call does O(N) mutable borrows to enforce an invariant that should already hold. This is O(N) work on every update, O(N^2) per session.

Additionally, the cycle check (line 237) is done BEFORE the update is applied, but uses self.would_create_cycle which walks current task.blocks. If the caller passes BOTH add_blocks AND add_blocked_by in the same call, cycle detection only checks each edge in isolation, not the combined graph. Two edges A -> B and B -> A added in one call would both pass the per-edge check while together forming a cycle.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Extract a Graph data structure with explicit add_edge / would_create_cycle / sync_reverse methods. Put the invariant (blocks[A] contains B iff blocked_by[B] contains A) in ONE place.
2. Run cycle detection AFTER staging all new edges, before committing, using a full DFS on the proposed graph.
3. Delete the 'suppress unused warning' dead code block (3.2 DRY/YAGNI violation).
4. Replace Vec<String> adjacency with HashSet<String> for O(1) contains and dedup.
5. Add fuzz test: generate random add_blocks/add_blocked_by pairs, assert graph consistency holds.
